title: Treasury Marketable and Non-Marketable Securities
summary: This is a navigation/index page on Treasury.gov that lists resources related to Treasury marketable securities auctions, non-marketable securities programs, and related regulations. The page provides links to auction schedules, query tools, and information about savings bonds, but contains no specific auction dates, results, or data. It does not contain any actionable market-moving information.
key_points: Page is a directory/index structure linking to Treasury auction resources rather than a specific document or data release
References Treasury Marketable Securities Tentative Auction Schedule for the next six months but does not include the actual schedule content
Links to Treasury Auction Query tool for downloading auction data in multiple formats
Describes Treasury STRIPS program and references debt data in Table V of the Monthly Statement of the Public Debt
Covers non-marketable securities including SLGS, Series EE, and Series I Savings Bonds
Provides links to Treasury Uniform Offering Circular (UOC) governing auction terms and conditions
why_it_matters: This page has no direct market impact as it contains no specific auction data, results, or policy information. It serves only as a navigation aid to Treasury resources. Researchers seeking actual auction schedules or results would need to follow the linked resources.
market_structure_context: This page references Treasury auction mechanisms and market structure elements (STRIPS, auction regulations, offering circulars) but provides no specific operational details. Treasury auctions are a core component of U.S. market structure.
notes: This source is a navigation/listing page on Treasury.gov that catalogs links to Treasury securities resources. It contains no specific auction dates, results, or market-moving information. The body text describes what resources are available (auction schedules, query tools, regulations, savings bonds) but does not include any actual data. Per CORE RULE 6, listing_page type should be treated as non-archiveable. Source has low informational value for a finance research archive. Classification as treasury_issuance is a loose fit since there is no actual issuance data present.

reasoning: Navigation/index page with no substantive content
No specific dates, numbers, or results provided
Listing page type per classification guidance - non-archiveable
